title: NFS Storage Mount Failure on Application Server Farm
symptoms: NFS mount points showing stale file handle errors on application servers
Application processes in D (uninterruptible sleep) state waiting for NFS I/O
NetApp storage controller showing successful failover to partner node
NFS clients not reconnecting after storage controller failover
df and ls commands hanging indefinitely on affected mount points
root_cause: NFS hard mount configuration without intr or timeo options prevented automatic reconnection after NetApp storage controller failover during firmware maintenance
resolution: Forced NFS unmount and remount with soft mount options on all affected servers, verified storage controller health, and updated NFS mount configuration to include reconnection parameters
preventive_actions: Update NFS mount configuration to use soft mounts with timeout values
Implement NFS mount health monitoring with automatic remount capability
Coordinate storage controller maintenance with application team notifications
Add NFS client reconnection testing to storage maintenance procedures
Create automated NFS mount recovery script for failover scenarios

timeline.event: NetApp storage controller begins firmware maintenance failover
NFS mounts become stale on all 12 application servers
Application processes begin hanging on NFS I/O operations
Alert fires for application server unresponsiveness
Chris Evans identifies NFS mount failure as root cause
Forced unmount and remount initiated on all servers
All NFS mounts restored, application processes recovering

embedding_summary: NFS mount failure on 12 application servers after NetApp storage controller failover during firmware maintenance. Hard mount configuration without timeout values prevented automatic reconnection. 35-minute outage affecting file upload and document processing. Resolved by forced remount with soft mount options.
